heading: Open container requirements
label: 154 Open container requirements
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 154, added , , ; amended , , ; , , ; , , ; , (b), (e), (g), , , established the national maximum speed limit, prior to repeal by , (3), , , applicable to State on 10th day following , except that if legislature was not in session on such date and chief executive officer declared before such date that legislature was not in session and that State preferred applicability date that was after date on which legislature would convene, applicable to State on 60th day following date on which legislature would next convene.Pub. Amendments2021—Subsec. (c)(1). , substituted “impaired” for “alcohol-impaired” in subpars. (A) and (B).Pub. L. 117–58, § 24106(a)Subsec. (c)(2). , substituted “2022” for “2012” in heading.Pub. L. 117–58, § 11131(a)(1)Subsec. (c)(2)(A). , added subpar. (A) and struck out former subpar. (A). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “On , and each October 1 thereafter, if a State has not enacted or is not enforcing an open container law described in subsection (b), the Secretary shall reserve an amount equal to 2.5 percent of the funds to be apportioned to the State on that date under each of paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 104(b) until the State certifies to the Secretary the means by which the State will use those reserved funds in accordance with subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) and paragraph (3).”Pub. (c)(1). , substituted “paragraphs (1), (2), and (4)” for “paragraphs (1), (3), and (4)”.Pub. L. 114–94, § 1446(a)(8)(A)Subsec. (c)(3)(A). , substituted “reserved” for “transferred”.Pub. (c)(5)(A). , substituted “under section 104(b)(1)” for “under section 104(b)(l)”.Pub. L. 114–94, § 1446(a)(8)(C)(ii)2012—Subsec. (c)(2). , added par. (2) and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “On , and each October 1 thereafter, if a State has not enacted or is not enforcing an open container law described in subsection (b), the Secretary shall transfer an amount equal to 3 percent of the funds apportioned to the State on that date under each of paragraphs (1), (3), and (4) of section 104(b) to the apportionment of the State under section 402 to be used or directed as described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of paragraph (1).”Pub. L. 112–141, § 1402(1)October 1, 2002Subsec. (c)(3). , added par. (3) and struck out former par. (3). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The amount to be transferred under paragraph (1) or (2) may be derived from one or more of the following:Pub. children.heading: .—Alcoholic beverage
children.label: 1 .—Alcoholic beverage
children.text: The term “alcoholic beverage” has the meaning given the term in section 158(c).

children.heading: .—Motor vehicle
children.label: 2 .—Motor vehicle
children.text: The term “motor vehicle” means a vehicle driven or drawn by mechanical power and manufactured primarily for use on public highways, but does not include a vehicle operated exclusively on a rail or rails.

children.label: A
children.text: that contains any amount of alcoholic beverage; and

children.label: i
children.text: that is open or has a broken seal; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the contents of which are partially removed.

children.heading: .—Passenger area
children.label: 4 .—Passenger area
children.text: The term “passenger area” shall have the meaning given the term by the Secretary by regulation.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: For the purposes of this section, each State shall have in effect a law that prohibits the possession of any open alcoholic beverage container, or the consumption of any alcoholic beverage, in the passenger area of any motor vehicle (including possession or consumption by the driver of the vehicle) located on a public highway, or the right-of-way of a public highway, in the State.

children.heading: .—Motor vehicles designed to transport many passengers
children.label: 2 .—Motor vehicles designed to transport many passengers
A
children.text: in the passenger area of a motor vehicle designed, maintained, or used primarily for the transportation of persons for compensation; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the living quarters of a house coach or house trailer,

children.label: A
children.text: to be used for impaired driving countermeasures; or

children.label: B
children.text: to be directed to State and local law enforcement agencies for enforcement of laws prohibiting driving while intoxicated or driving under the influence and other related laws (including regulations), including the purchase of equipment, the training of officers, and the use of additional personnel for specific impaired driving countermeasures, dedicated to enforcement of the laws (including regulations).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: On , and each October 1 thereafter, in the case of a State described in clause (ii), the Secretary shall reserve an amount equal to 2.5 percent of the funds to be apportioned to the State on that date under each of paragraphs (1) and (2) of section 104(b) until the State certifies to the Secretary the means by which the State will use those reserved funds in accordance with subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1), and paragraph (3).October 1, 2021

children.label: I
children.text: that has not enacted or is not enforcing an open container law described in subsection (b); and

children.label: II
children.text: for which the Secretary determined for the prior fiscal year that the State had not enacted or was not enforcing an open container law described in subsection (b).

children.label: i
children.text: transfer the reserved funds identified by the State for use as described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1) to the apportionment of the State under section 402; and

children.label: ii
children.text: release the reserved funds identified by the State as described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: A State may elect to use all or a portion of the funds reserved under paragraph (2) for activities eligible under section 148.

children.heading: .—State departments of transportation
children.label: B .—State departments of transportation
children.text: If the State makes an election under subparagraph (A), the funds shall be transferred to the department of transportation of the State, which shall be responsible for the administration of the funds.

children.heading: .—Federal share
children.label: 4 .—Federal share
children.text: The Federal share of the cost of a project carried out with funds transferred under paragraph (1) or (2), or used under paragraph (3), shall be 100 percent.

children.label: A
children.text: The apportionment of the State under section 104(b)(1).

children.label: B
children.text: The apportionment of the State under section 104(b)(2).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: If the Secretary transfers under this subsection any funds to the apportionment of a State under section 402 for a fiscal year, the Secretary shall transfer an amount, determined under subparagraph (B), of obligation authority distributed for the fiscal year to the State for Federal-aid highways and highway safety construction programs for carrying out projects under section 402.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of funds transferred under subparagraph (A) to the apportionment of the State under section 402 for the fiscal year, by

children.label: I
children.text: the amount of obligation authority distributed for the fiscal year to the State for Federal-aid highways and highway safety construction programs, bears to

children.label: II
children.text: the total of the sums apportioned to the State for Federal-aid highways and highway safety construction programs (excluding sums not subject to any obligation limitation) for the fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Limitation on applicability of obligation limitation
children.label: 7 .—Limitation on applicability of obligation limitation
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no limitation on the total of obligations for highway safety programs under section 402 shall apply to funds transferred under this subsection to the apportionment of a State under such section.
